title: Surveillance Footage of BU Bridge 11/16/23 (Boston Police Department)
Surveillance Footage of BU Bridge 11/16/23 (Cambridge Police Department)
2026 Dallas County Texas DPS Flock Safety ALPR Audit

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the agency's Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) system:

Organization Audit in Flock, from April 1, 2026 to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency. Please include, for each search, the date and time, the searching user or unit, and the reason or justification entered in the audit trail.

Network Audit in Flock, for the same period. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the agency's Flock network by any agency in the Flock System. Please include, for each search, the date and time, the searching agency, and the reason or justification entered.

A current list of network share settings, including: (a) Flock networks shared with this agency ("shared with me"); (b) networks this agency is sharing with others ("networks I'm sharing"); and (c) any sharing with or access by other state, out-of-state, or federal agencies — including ICE, CBP, or DHS — and the date any such setting was enabled or disabled.

The agency's General Order, directive, or written policy governing ALPR/Flock use, including operator access, the search-justification requirement, data-retention period, audit procedures, and data sharing.

Please provide the audit and share-setting records in their original electronic, machine-readable format (CSV or spreadsheet). I request that personally identifying information of non-employee subjects be redacted rather than the records withheld. Please do not redirect me to an online transparency portal in lieu of producing these records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ryan Becker

title: 2026 Frisco Texas Flock Safety ALPR Audit

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act (Tex. Gov't Code ch. 552), I request copies of the following records concerning the Frisco Police Department's use of Flock Safety automated license plate readers (ALPRs). To keep this request narrow and limit cost, the audit reports below are limited to the time period specified.

Time period for the audit reports (items 1–2): April 1, 2026 to the date this request is processed.  

Records requested:
1.	Organization Audit in Flock. The report covering the time period above. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency. Please include the searching user, date/time, and the reason/justification entered for each search.
2.	Network Audit in Flock. The report covering the time period above. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization's Flock network by any agency in the Flock System. Please include the searching agency, date/time, and the reason/justification entered for each search.
3.	A current list of network share settings, including:
a.	Flock networks shared with the Frisco Police Department (“shared with me”);
b.	Networks the Frisco Police Department is sharing with others (“networks I'm sharing”);
c.	Any statewide, out-of-state, or federal sharing that is enabled — including data shared with the Texas Department of Public Safety, and any access by or sharing with federal agencies such as ICE, CBP, or DHS — and the date any such setting was enabled or disabled.
4.	The department's General Order, directive, or written policy governing the use of Flock Safety / ALPR technology, including rules on operator access, the search-justification requirement, the data-retention period, audit procedures, and data sharing.
5.	Any records reflecting access to Frisco's cameras or data by Flock Safety personnel (for example, for product demonstrations, debugging, or support) during the time period above.
Format: Please provide records in their original electronic, machine-readable format — for example, CSV or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your online transparency portal in lieu of producing these records.

Please release all non-exempt portions and cite the specific statutory exception for anything withheld; reasonable redactions required by law are acceptable. If any part is unclear, please contact me for clarification rather than denying the request.

Fees: If there are any fees, please inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling the request. I am willing to pay reasonable fees up to $100; if the estimate is higher, please contact me so I can narrow the date range.

I look forward to your response within 10 business days, as the statute requires. Thank you for your assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ryan Becker

title: Waco Crime Free Housing Training Records

communications.communication:
To Whom it may concern,
Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:
1. A list of all properties in Waco who have completed the Crime Free Multi-Housing training 
2. Any and all training materials used by Waco Police Department for the Crime Free Multi-Housing Training Program
3. Calls for service records from Waco PD to properties enrolled in the Waco Crime Free Training Program. 
4. Crime Free lease used in Waco 

Kindly,
Jenny Wadhwa

title: Austin Crime Free Housing Training Property Records

communications.communication:
To Whom it may concern,
Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:
1. A list of all properties in Austin who have completed the 8-hour Phase 1 Crime Free Housing training by Officer John Gabrielson 

Kindly,
Jenny Wadhwa
